title: Felix and the Sleepy Star
synopsis: Felix feels scared at bedtime until he discovers a lonely star outside his window who needs his help to fall asleep too, teaching him that being brave can help both himself and others find peaceful sleep.

pages.text: Felix was ready for bed, but he felt scared. His room looked dark and big. "I don't want to go to sleep," Felix told Mama.

illustration.description: A young Black boy with curly hair wearing blue pajamas stands in his cozy bedroom doorway, looking worried. His mother, a warm Black woman with natural hair in a colorful head wrap, kneels beside him with caring eyes. The room has soft yellow lighting from a bedside lamp, with toys scattered on a textured carpet.
illustration.alt: Felix looking scared in his pajamas while his mother comforts him

pages.text: Mama gave Felix a big hug. "What makes you feel scared?" she asked gently. Felix pointed to the shadows on his wall. "They look scary," he whispered.

illustration.description: Mama hugs Felix close in the warm bedroom. Gentle shadows from tree branches outside create soft patterns on the bright yellow walls. Felix's bed has a colorful quilt with star patterns. The scene feels safe and loving despite Felix's worried expression.
illustration.alt: Mother hugging Felix while he points at shadows on the wall

pages.text: "Let's look out the window together," Mama said. They walked to the window and saw a little star twinkling all alone in the big dark sky.

illustration.description: Felix and his mother stand at a large window with white curtains. Outside, a single bright yellow star twinkles against a deep purple night sky. Felix's worried face begins to show curiosity as he presses his small hands against the glass.
illustration.alt: Felix and his mother looking out the window at a lonely star

pages.text: "The little star looks lonely," Felix said. "Maybe it feels scared too." Mama smiled. "You're very kind to notice. What could help the star feel better?"

illustration.description: Close-up of Felix's face showing empathy and growing confidence. His brown eyes are wide with understanding. Through the window behind him, the star seems to twinkle more brightly. The illustration uses warm oranges and yellows to show Felix's changing mood from scared to caring.
illustration.alt: Felix looking thoughtful and caring as he thinks about the lonely star

pages.text: Felix felt brave now. "I'll sing to the star!" he said. He sang a soft lullaby: "Twinkle, little star, you're safe where you are. Close your sleepy eyes, underneath the skies."

illustration.description: Felix stands confidently at the window, mouth open in song, with his arms raised expressively. The star outside glows warmly, and other smaller stars begin to appear around it. Musical notes float in the air around Felix, rendered in soft pastels against the construction paper-textured background.
illustration.alt: Felix singing bravely to the star with musical notes around him

pages.text: The star seemed to twinkle happily, and more stars appeared around it. Felix felt happy and sleepy too. "Now we can all sleep together," he yawned, crawling into his cozy bed.

illustration.description: Felix lies peacefully in his bed with a content smile, his curly hair spread on the pillow. Through the window, many twinkling stars fill the now-friendly night sky. His room glows with soft moonlight, and the shadows that once scared him now look like gentle shapes. The overall mood is serene and safe.
illustration.alt: Felix sleeping peacefully in bed with stars visible through his window

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Felix feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Felix felt scared because his room looked dark and big, and he didn't want to go to sleep.

questions.question: What did Felix see outside his window?
questions.explanation: Felix and Mama saw a little star twinkling all alone in the big dark sky.

questions.question: How did Felix help the lonely star?
questions.explanation: Felix sang a soft lullaby to help the star feel better and less lonely.

questions.question: How did Felix feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: After helping the star, Felix felt happy and sleepy, ready to go to bed peacefully.

pages.text: Felix felt _____ at bedtime until he saw the lonely _____.
Felix became _____ and sang a _____ to help the star feel better.
